verdict: NOT DETECTED at v0. Across all four channels the real structure is SMOOTHER than the time-shuffled null (shuffled scores higher), not peakier.
diagnosis: CLIP-similarity between moments is dominated by smooth visual/topic continuity; it contains no sharp forward-pointing spikes that stand out above noise. Similarity is also symmetric and atemporal, so it cannot by itself prove a DIRECTED reference->gratification. To detect directed binding we need a PREDICTIVE instrument (does moment i reduce uncertainty about a specific later moment j) — a trained V-JEPA/CPC future-latent model or a cross-modal moment-retrieval model — not raw cosine similarity. The cheap proxy provably falls short, which is exactly what Step 1 is for.
